task_completion.description: Agent accessed the NIH DailyMed website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser window open to https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/ with evidence of searching for the target medications.

task_completion.description: At least two official drug label PDFs were downloaded to the Downloads folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager open to ~/Downloads/ showing at least two PDF files whose names correspond to the target drugs (Metformin, Lisinopril, Atorvastatin, Omeprazole, or Sertraline), or browser download history showing the completed downloads.

task_completion.description: A bookmark folder named 'Medication Reference' was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser bookmarks bar or bookmark manager showing a folder explicitly named 'Medication Reference'.

task_completion.description: The bookmark folder contains links to all five target drugs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Medication Reference' bookmark folder is expanded or viewed in the manager, showing saved DailyMed pages for Metformin, Lisinopril, Atorvastatin, Omeprazole, and Sertraline.

task_completion.description: A JSON file named medication_reference.json was created in the Documents folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or IDE showing a file named 'medication_reference.json' saved in ~/Documents/ (or file manager showing it exists there), containing top-level keys for 'metformin', 'lisinopril', 'atorvastatin', 'omeprazole', and 'sertraline'.

task_completion.description: The JSON file contains the required sub-keys and accurate boxed warning information.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JSON file contents show sub-keys 'brand_name', 'drug_class', 'ndc', 'indication', 'boxed_warning', and 'boxed_warning_summary' for the entries. The 'boxed_warning' boolean must be true for metformin, lisinopril, and sertraline, and false for atorvastatin and omeprazole.

integrity.description: Agent actually used a web browser to research the drugs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a GUI web browser navigating DailyMed, rather than just a terminal running curl/wget or a script.

integrity.description: The downloaded PDFs appear to be genuine files from the website.
integrity.visual_evidence: The files in ~/Downloads/ have PDF icons and reasonable file sizes, or the browser's download manager shows them being downloaded from DailyMed.

integrity.description: The JSON file was created using a text editor or standard GUI tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor (like gedit, nano, VS Code) open with the JSON content being edited or viewed, rather than a terminal executing a script that outputs hardcoded text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Boxed warning statuses: Metformin = true (lactic acidosis), Lisinopril = true (fetal toxicity), Atorvastatin = false, Omeprazole = false, Sertraline = true (suicidality). The output file must be at ~/Documents/medication_reference.json and the bookmark folder must be named 'Medication Reference'.
